name: infranodus-graph-view
description: Knowledge graph for your text and code: visualize connections, find gaps, generate context-aware AI prompts.
author: InfraNodus SAS
license: Copyright (c) 2024-2026 InfraNodus SAS. All rights reserved.

infranodus-graph-view.apiKey.description: InfraNodus API key. Get one from infranodus.com → Settings → API. Stored in VS Code settings.json — use Settings Sync carefully.

enumItemLabels: Auto (text for prose files, code-architecture graph for code files)
Parsed Code (architecture reference graph: functions, classes, variables)
enumDescriptions: Picks per file based on extension. Prose files (.md, .txt, .rst, …) use Parsed Text. Code files (.ts, .py, .go, …) use Parsed Code. Unknown extensions fall back to Parsed Text.
Extract only prose-like content: comments, docstrings, plain text from HTML/Markdown, string literals that look like sentences, and [[wikilinks]]. Skips code scaffolding.

Build an architecture reference graph: nodes are functions, classes, methods, and exported variables; edges are containment and references. Clicking a node in the graph jumps the editor to that symbol's definition. Requires a language server for the file (TS/JS, Python, Go, etc.).
Send the entire file as-is, with simple block compression so code structures fit on single lines. Useful when you want every token analyzed.
infranodus-graph-view.contentToSend.description: What content to send for analysis. Auto picks per file: text extraction for prose (.md, .txt, …) and a code-architecture graph for code (.ts, .py, …). Parsed text extracts comments, plain text, and [[wikilinks]]. Parsed code builds an architecture graph of functions/classes/variables and their references (click a node to jump to its definition). Full file contents sends everything.

enumDescriptions: All meaningful words become graph nodes alongside any [[wiki links]] and #hashtags. Most inclusive — best for free-form prose, comments, or text without explicit markup.

If the text contains [[wiki links]] or #hashtags, only those become nodes (the rest is treated as connective tissue). Otherwise falls back to all words. Useful for documents authored with intentional markup.
Only [[wiki links]] and #hashtags become nodes — everything else is ignored. Use for curated ontologies, manually-tagged notes, and tightly-scoped knowledge graphs.
infranodus-graph-view.partOfSpeechToProc.description: Controls which tokens in the text become nodes in the knowledge graph. Note: this setting is overridden in Parsed Code mode, which always treats symbol names as atomic tokens regardless of this value.

infranodus-graph-view.stopwords.description: Words to exclude from graph node generation. The defaults filter common code keywords so they don't dominate the graph when analyzing source files. Add domain-specific noise words (e.g. "figure", "page") to clean up analysis of papers or reports. Note: this setting is ignored in Parsed Code mode, which uses an empty stopword list.

infranodus-graph-view.modelToUse.description: AI model used both for AI-driven topic naming inside the knowledge graph and for the in-extension AI buttons (Question, Idea, Summary, Context, Bridge Gap). The list mirrors what's available via /api/v1/aiModels on your InfraNodus account.

infranodus-graph-view.customAIPrompt.default: Generate an idea that uses the current context and the graph structure below:
infranodus-graph-view.customAIPrompt.description: Prompt for AI: Develop Mode — the prompt used when you click the "Develop" button to generate new ideas grounded in the current graph.

infranodus-graph-view.useTranscendMode.description: Use "Transcend" mode for the AI: Develop action. When enabled, clicking the "Develop" button asks the AI to look beyond the current discourse — surfacing ideas that connect your existing graph to concepts outside of it, rather than developing what's already there. Useful for lateral, outside-the-box exploration and cross-domain bridging. When disabled, Develop stays grounded in the current graph and uses the Custom AI Prompt above.

infranodus-graph-view.customAIChatPrompt.default: Use the graph and context below to start a discussion. Answer follow-up questions referring to the graph structure when relevant.
infranodus-graph-view.customAIChatPrompt.description: Prompt for AI: Chat Mode — the prompt used when you click the "Chat" button to start a discussion grounded in the current graph and context.

infranodus-graph-view.theme.description: Color theme for the InfraNodus graph view and extension UI. Auto follows the current VS Code color theme.

infranodus-graph-view.apiUrl.description: InfraNodus API URL (default: https://infranodus.com). Change only if you run a self-hosted InfraNodus instance.

infranodus-graph-view.graphUrl.description: InfraNodus Graph rendering URL (default: https://graph.infranodus.com). Change only if you self-host the graph renderer.

commands.title: InfraNodus Graph: Paste Graph in a File
InfraNodus Graph: Paste Selected Graph in a File
InfraNodus Graph: Visualize Diff as Graph
InfraNodus Graph: Visualize Whole Repo Diff as Graph

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
